frame_analyses.description: Initial setup with suture and needle holder visible. Penrose drain positioned on platform with marked locations.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good starting position with instruments ready

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned to pass through the first mark on the Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precise needle positioning approaching the marked target

frame_analyses.description: Suture being passed through both marks on the Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears to be placed accurately through both marked locations

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot with right hand manipulation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper initiation of surgeon's knot with double wrap preparation

frame_analyses.description: Completing the surgeon's knot first throw with proper double wrap
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Excellent surgeon's knot formation with secure double throw

frame_analyses.description: Initiating second throw with hand switch, now using left hand
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching demonstrated between throws

frame_analyses.description: Beginning third throw with another hand switch back to right hand
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct hand switching pattern maintained for final throw

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends being cut with scissors to complete the task
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cutting of both suture tails completing the procedure

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw executed well
Single throw with proper hand switch from throw 1
Single throw with proper hand switch from throw 2

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gap after knot tightening

technique_summary: Excellent performance demonstrating proper surgeon's knot followed by two single throws with correct hand switching. Suture placement was accurate and knot appears secure with good drain closure.
improvement_suggestions: Continue practicing to reduce completion time while maintaining quality
Focus on even more precise suture placement to minimize deviation penalties
